seoTitle: Ferny Hills vs Fielding — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Ferny Hills and Fielding. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Ferny Hills vs Fielding: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Ferny Hills and Fielding.
heading: Ferny Hills vs Fielding — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Ferny Hills, Queensland and Fielding, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Ferny Hills has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Fielding (33/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Ferny Hills or Fielding safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Ferny Hills has a flood risk score of 29/100, while Fielding scores 59/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Ferny Hills scores 32/100 for bushfire risk. Fielding scores 54/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Ferny Hills or Fielding?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Ferny Hills has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.45% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
